title: First Impact
wikitext:
[]: First Impact             Quote Post by Nephilim » Fri Aug 27, 2004 6:15 pm I was looking at some screen-caps of the episode 7, when a text about the second impact is shown: Read carefully the text titled "2. The fall of massive meteorite"... The meteorite was less than 4 inches in diameter???? It's velocity was 95% of the speed of light???? WHO THE HELL COULD BUY THAT CRAP ???? :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Aug 27, 2004 6:18 pm I'm quite sure that isn't referring to the meteorite that created the moon Nephilim. Unless of course the moon was created sometime after 1971...

[]:             Quote Post by Treeves056 » Fri Aug 27, 2004 6:31 pm Wouldnt it burn up in the atmoshere if it was that small

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Aug 27, 2004 7:04 pm Treeves056 wrote: Wouldnt it burn up in the atmoshere if it was that small

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Aug 27, 2004 7:22 pm thewayneiac wrote: But one of the points they were making was that nothing with any mass at all could be going anywhere near that fast.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Aug 27, 2004 7:49 pm Dave wrote: thewayneiac wrote: But one of the points they were making was that nothing with any mass at all could be going anywhere near that fast.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Aug 27, 2004 8:00 pm No, they both have easily (well... with modern instruments anywa) measureable mass. Most other subatomic particles can also be accelerated up to and over 95% of the speed of light using particle accelerators.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Aug 27, 2004 8:24 pm Dave wrote: No, they both have easily (well... with modern instruments anywa) measureable mass. Most other subatomic particles can also be accelerated up to and over 95% of the speed of light using particle accelerators.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Aug 27, 2004 9:50 pm thewayneiac wrote: O.K., but they are made of energy, there is still no way that anything made of matter, like a meteor or a spaceship for instance, could go that fast. I do remember that the objection to matter going at or near the speed of light is that photons and other things that do go the speed of light exist at all points along their path simultaneously. Therefore matter going the speed of light would have infinite mass.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Aug 27, 2004 10:02 pm Well, there aren't many circumstances that would make a meteor go that close to the speed of light, but it is possible. And it is theoretically possible for something that small to do that much damage. This is the principle behind rail guns. If something is traveling at extremely high speeds, it has insane amounts of kinetic energy, and could cause that much damage with ease. If it doesn't make sense, remember that its a cover story concocted within a fictional world. it doesn't have to be 100% scientifically accurate.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Aug 27, 2004 10:05 pm I suppose it's possible... maybe it slingshotted around fifty black holes... in a row. I seriously doubt the people left surviving gave a damn in the years after.

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Sat Aug 28, 2004 12:23 am Even if it's possible to an object to travel that fast... It's just a 4 inches rock !!!! That could only make a big hole in the ground !!! not kill a billion people !!! How could the whole world buy that ??? Well, but that's juts me... :roll: I made this topic to show you that screencap, so all can say what you think

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Sat Aug 28, 2004 12:29 am it DID just crash in antartica. the WAVES are what killed peopl

[]: Nailed it.             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Sat Aug 28, 2004 1:04 am ShoKenju nailed it. The Second Impact harmed next to nobody, the fact that it's heat melted the polar ice caps was the problem. This caused flooding across the world, killing off half the human race and reshaping the world as we knew it. Again, this is a coverup, and I'll bet if anybody did figure out it was a hoax, SEELE would have pulled some strings and seen them taken care of. Also the hoax didn't have to cover their butts for long, just untill they got third IMpact underway. By then it really wouldn't have mattered.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Aug 28, 2004 1:11 am The initial aftermath was not what caused half the human population to perish. The massive tidal waves and flooding probably killed a hefty sum, the mass chaos more than likely caused a breakdown of numerous services and such. Anarchy ensues. And as the stuffy teacher says, the second impact did lead to a number of wars and ethnic conflicts, as well as starvation and famine, etc etc.

[]: Re: Nailed it.             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sat Aug 28, 2004 2:53 pm Olin of Xephon wrote: ShoKenju nailed it. The Second Impact harmed next to nobody, the fact that it's heat melted the polar ice caps was the problem. This caused flooding across the world, killing off half the human race and reshaping the world as we knew it.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sun Aug 29, 2004 4:22 pm This is all good as far as it goes, but 2I did more than just melt the ice caps and cause tidal waves. It changed the tilt of the Earth's axis, (shifting it to a straight up and down angle, resulting in the elimination of seasons). Something that catastrophic would probably kill billions of people outright, even before the tidal waves and chaos.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Aug 29, 2004 8:12 pm thewayneiac wrote: This is all good as far as it goes, but 2I did more than just melt the ice caps and cause tidal waves. It changed the tilt of the Earth's axis, (shifting it to a straight up and down angle, resulting in the elimination of seasons). Something that catastrophic would probably kill billions of people outright, even before the tidal waves and chaos.

[]: The Fiction in science Fiction...             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Sun Aug 29, 2004 11:57 pm I never thought the earth's axis had been completely disrupted. Obviously there had been A change. But I always assued it was maybe a 0.000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000002 percent shift thus why Japan, or what's left of it, we don't see much, but a lot of it is obviously flooded, as pointed out, Japan should no longer exist, is now more of a trpoical island. I didn't think Fuyutski meant there where no longer season, the climate of Japan was transformed, and thus every season felt like summer. (As a citizen of Florida I can relate to that idea) Also, it's possible while Japan was floded originally, isn't it possible that it was rebuilt? massive damns, and a drainage system used to re shape the earth or relocate the ocean come to mind as (almost) possible solutions.

[]:             Quote Post by NeonZ » Wed Sep 01, 2004 9:22 am Obviously there had been A change. But I always assued it was maybe a 0.000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000002 percent shift thus why Japan, or what's left of it, we don't see much, but a lot of it is obviously flooded, as pointed out, Japan should no longer exist, is now more of a trpoical island.
